content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 16, 1990


Vash Webster
Valley Insurance Program
448 West Shaw Avenue
Fresno, CA 93704

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-423

Dear Mr. Webster:

This is in response to your June 18, 1990 letter seeking
confirmation of your agency's filing requirements under the
conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform
Act.1/

It is your understanding that your agency, Valley Insurance
Program, is a local government agency not required to forward
statements of economic interest to the Fair Political Practices
Commission (the "Commission").

You are correct in your understanding. Pursuant to Governement
Code Section 87500 (k), the Commission is electing not to act as
your agency's filing officer. All statements filed by your
agency's designated employees, as specified in your agency's
conflict of interest code, are to be filed and maintained with
your agency. Your letter does indicate two possible locations,
either in the office of the administrator or the financial records
center. We do ask that you determine which of these two locations
will be the filing officer, and include that location in the
conflict of interest code file. This will ensure that all
designated persons will know exactly where to file their
statement. Any confusion as to where a designated employee should
file their statements may result in a delay and a possible late
filing penalty.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. I-90-423
Page 2


Should you require further assistance with your conflict of
interest code, please do not hesitate to contact the Legal
Division at (916) 322-5901. If you have questions regarding the
Form 730, please contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel


By: Cheryl A. Hoff
Staff Services Analyst
Legal Division

SH:CAH:I90423

sections.question_synthetic: Does Valley Insurance Program, as a local government agency, have filing requirements under the conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding whether to submit statements of economic interest to the FPPC?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Valley Insurance Program is correctly understood to be a local government agency not required to forward statements of economic interest to the FPPC. Under Government Code Section 87500(k), the FPPC is electing not to act as the agency's filing officer, and all statements must be filed and maintained with the agency itself. The agency should designate a single filing location (either the office of the administrator or the financial records center) in its conflict of interest code to avoid confusion and potential late filing penalties.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter responding to June 18, 1990 request. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers, but clear Q&A structure present. Document discusses Government Code Section 87500(k) and conflict of interest code filing procedures. Reference to Form 730 also included.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Valley Insurance Program, as a local government agency, have filing requirements under the conflict of interest code provisions of the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding whether to submit statements of economic interest to the FPPC?

CONCLUSION: Valley Insurance Program is correctly understood to be a local government agency not required to forward statements of economic interest to the FPPC. Under Government Code Section 87500(k), the FPPC is electing not to act as the agency's filing officer, and all statements must be filed and maintained with the agency itself. The agency should designate a single filing location (either the office of the administrator or the financial records center) in its conflict of interest code to avoid confusion and potential late filing penalties.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirmation letter to Valley Insurance Program advising that as a local government agency, it is not required to file statements of economic interest with the FPPC and should instead maintain all filings internally at a designated location specified in its conflict of interest code.
